# conftest.py
import pytest
from sqlalchemy import create_engine
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker

engine = create_engine('sqlite:///:memory:')
Session = sessionmaker(engine)

@pytest.fixture(scope='session')  # BUG: should be function scope for per-test isolation
def db():
    s = Session()
    yield s
    s.close()

# test_users.py
def test_create_user_unique(db):
    db.execute('CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users(id int primary key, email text)')
    db.execute("INSERT INTO users(id, email) VALUES (1, 'a@b.com')")
    count = db.execute('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users').scalar()
    assert count == 1, f"expected 1 user after create, got {count}"

def test_create_user_duplicate(db):
    db.execute("INSERT INTO users(id, email) VALUES (2, 'a@b.com')")
    db.execute("INSERT INTO users(id, email) VALUES (3, 'a@b.com')")
expected_fix_hint: Change fixture scope to 'function' or wrap each test in a transaction that rolls back in teardown.
